beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check on fiddle leaf fig and other plants in the apartment.
beats.reasoning: Tomas starts his day at home in Wallingford with his morning routine—coffee and plant care are core to his character and help him ease into a rainy Tuesday.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland, delayed by SR-520 eastbound closure; takes longer route via I-5 and I-405.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon, so Tomas leaves early to beat the closure window. Even so, the detour adds 15–20 min to his normal commute; he budgets carefully given his transit-engineer mindset.

beats.activity: Work at transit agency: morning meetings on Light Rail closure logistics, design reviews, and documentation.
beats.reasoning: Tomas settles into work mode, focused on his Light Rail closure report—yesterday's momentum carries through. The rainy weather keeps him at his desk; no outdoor site visits today.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby café in Kirkland; eats indoors due to steady rain.
beats.reasoning: Tomas grabs lunch locally in Kirkland rather than venturing out in the rain. A quick, low-key meal fits his mood—energy is moderate after yesterday's excitement.

beats.activity: Return to work: afternoon design reviews, closure documentation, and stakeholder prep.
beats.reasoning: Tomas stays focused at work through the afternoon, wrapping up the closure report and preparing for stakeholder meetings. The rain and energy at 45/100 keep him grounded at his desk.

beats.activity: Drive home to Seattle; rain has cleared but roads still damp.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute back to Seattle; the rain window (7am–2pm) has passed, so conditions are improving. Tomas takes a direct route via I-405 and I-5.

beats.activity: Errand: stop at QFC for groceries and brewing supplies on the way home.
beats.reasoning: A quick stop near home for supplies—Tomas is in the habit of picking up brewing ingredients. Keeps the errand local and efficient.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner at home: simple pasta and a light beer from his recent batch.
beats.reasoning: Tomas cooks at home, a reflective and grounding ritual. He pairs it with one of his own brews—a small personal win after a steady day.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: review brewing notes, water plants, organize notes on the Light Rail closure for tomorrow.
beats.reasoning: Tomas winds down at home with low-key activities—brewing notes, plant care, and work prep. His moderate energy and perfectionist nature lead him to organize and reflect rather than go out.
Tomas goes to bed after a quiet evening, getting a solid 8.5 hours of sleep before Wednesday—recovery after yesterday's World Cup excitement and today's steady work focus.

diary: Woke up to grey skies and that steady drizzle that makes you want to stay in bed. Checked on my fiddle leaf fig—it's looking happier than it was last week, which felt like a small win. The SR-520 closure threw everything into chaos this morning; I ended up sitting in traffic on I-405 longer than usual, which meant I was already in work-mode by the time I got to Kirkland. Meetings back-to-back about the Light Rail logistics kept my head spinning. Grabbed lunch at Café Veloce and just sat there watching the rain, grateful for the quiet. Afternoon was more of the same—design reviews, documentation, stakeholder emails. The drive home felt easier once the rain stopped, though the roads were still slick. Picked up some groceries and brewing supplies at QFC, then made a simple pasta dinner with one of my recent batches. Spent the evening tending to the plants, reviewing my brewing notes, and organizing tomorrow's closure prep. There's something grounding about that routine.
thought_process: Today was one of those days where external chaos—the bridge closure, the rain—shaped everything. I normally have a smoother commute, so that detour put me in a reactive headspace from the start. Work was dense with logistics and stakeholder management around the Light Rail closure, which kept me focused but drained. I think I leaned harder into the routine stuff tonight—plants, brewing notes, organizing—because I needed to feel like I had control over something small and tangible after a day of managing larger infrastructure problems. The café lunch was deliberate; I needed a mental break from the office energy. By evening, I was less frazzled and more reflective, which is why the brewing notes felt meditative rather than like another task.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
